
Two arXiv papers from the RSS feed meet ≥3 inclusion criteria with meaningful harness-layer contributions. Grok social signals are either already in the README or show signs of synthetic/low-confidence evidence. The top candidates are a memory state-management harness paper and an artifact-based agent execution framework paper.

## Suggestions

- [Memanto: Typed Semantic Memory with Information-Theoretic Retrieval for Long-Horizon Agents](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.22085) — Proposes a harness-layer typed semantic memory system with information-theoretic retrieval for managing agent state across long-horizon tasks.
  Section: Research Papers > Context & State Management | Criteria: Manages tool dispatch, context handling, state management, or safety constraints at the harness layer, Defines or implements the execution loop, scaffolding, or runtime environment that hosts an AI agent, Empirical or production evidence of harness design improving reliability, safety, or performance

- [An Artifact-based Agent Framework for Adaptive and Reproducible Medical Image Processing](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.21936) — Introduces an artifact-centric agent execution framework with structured state management and reproducible harness-mediated task execution.
  Section: Agent Execution Frameworks | Criteria: Defines or implements the execution loop, scaffolding, or runtime environment that hosts an AI agent, Manages tool dispatch, context handling, state management, or safety constraints at the harness layer, Evaluates or benchmarks agent behavior through controlled harness-mediated interactions
